results.prompt:
Generate one game-ready isometric flat-top hex terrain tile concept image.
Use the base image strictly as the geometry template: same camera angle, same centered composition, same explicit top-edge bevel band, same vertical side-wall layout, same full hex silhouette.
The bevel area must contain natural hand-painted detail, not a clean vector edge.
Do not draw any additional closed outline on the upper top surface inner rim. The top face should stay mostly clean and readable.
On the lower visible bevel edges and side-wall vertical seams, add very thin semi-transparent dark hand-drawn strokes, uneven and painterly, integrated into the art.
Add a few short random vertical hairline cracks, notches, and tiny chips on visible side walls, similar to the edge crack reference image. They should feel like painted material detail, not a postprocess overlay.
Terrain material: mossy grass top, olive green turf, tiny weeds, small stones, natural worn soil patches; side walls are mossy stacked earth-stone blocks.
Style: low-saturation painterly fantasy tactics/board-game tile, crisp small-scale details, hand-painted natural edge wear, no heavy black border.
Avoid: no labels, no text, no arrows, no UI overlay, no red guide marks, no thick cartoon black outline, no extra closed black line on the top inner rim, do not cover the top face with a programmatic outline.
Plain white background is acceptable. Single tile only.
